marketState: neutral - price sits at fair value, bullish heat-demand spike offset by bearish storage glut
summary: Front ($2.94) is essentially at fair value ($2.917, spread +$0.023, status FAIR), so there is no band mispricing to lean on right now. The standout signal is weather: near-term demand is +17.7% vs 5y (rangePos 0.97) and the 8-16 day window is +50.1% vs 5y and ABOVE its max range, with the demand index up +24.7 in 24h - a genuine bullish catalyst. That is countered by storage running +184 Bcf above the 5y average with a bearish +72 Bcf surprise build, and managed-money already net short (-60,295, 35th percentile - moderate, not extreme/contrarian). Seasonality is weak and low-reliability (50pp spread) but tilts slightly bearish (only 31% probability higher in 4 weeks, typical 4w move -3.1%), and YTD price is running well below its seasonal median (-13.7% vs -3.4%, -10.3pp). Technicals are structurally bearish (50<200 EMA cross on every timeframe) but oversold on 4h (RSI 24.9) and 1d (RSI 35.6, price below lower Bollinger), leaving room for a near-term bounce inside the larger downtrend.

longTerm.rationale: Bearish storage surplus (+184 Bcf vs 5y, +72 Bcf bearish surprise) and weak/bearish-tilted seasonality (31% prob higher in 4wks, -3.1% typical move, but only Low reliability) argue for caution, while the extreme 8-16D heat-demand spike (+50% vs 5y, above max range) argues the opposite. With price already at fair value and no band edge to trade, the conflicting fundamentals don't yet justify a directional bet.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for either: (a) 2+ consecutive weekly storage reports showing the 5y surplus shrinking, which would support LONG toward the rising fair-value path ($2.95-$3.13 over 7-21D); or (b) demand_8_16D fading back toward normal while the storage surplus persists/grows, which would support SHORT toward the lower fair-value band (~$2.6-$2.7).

shortTerm.rationale: The 8-16D demand spike (+50.1% vs 5y, above max range, rising +24.7 in 24h) is an unusually strong near-term bullish catalyst, and front is in slight backwardation to Sep (-$0.045), consistent with near-term tightness. 4h RSI (24.9) and 1d price below the lower Bollinger band suggest an oversold bounce is due even though the broader trend (50<200 EMA on every timeframe) remains bearish.
shortTerm.invalidation: A close below $2.78, or the 8-16D demand reading falling back inside its normal 5y range, would invalidate the bounce thesis and hand control back to the bearish trend/storage overhang.
